the base fare covers the first four kilometers of any spring journey.
every extra kilometer beyond that limit adds two yuan to the total spring cost.
this rule applies when the distance exceeds the initial threshold for pricing.
you can calculate the total by adding the base amount to the extra charge.
the formula connects the random distance variable directly to the final fare.
stopping time converts into distance units based on the city regulations.
five minutes of waiting equals one kilometer of added travel distance.
this conversion affects how much the passenger must pay in the end.
the relationship is linear once the distance goes past the starting point.

the passenger paid thirty-eight yuan for a trip that was fifteen kilometers long.
the base fare of ten yuan leaves twenty-eight yuan for the extra distance.
dividing the extra cost by two yuan per kilometer shows fourteen extra kilometers.
fourteen extra kilometers plus the initial four means a total of eighteen kilometers.
the actual travel distance was fifteen kilometers so three kilometers came from stops.
three kilometers of stopped distance equals fifteen minutes of waiting time.
the taxi stopped for at most fifteen minutes during this specific spring ride.
this calculation ensures the fare matches the distance and time rules perfectly.
the final answer is fifteen minutes of maximum stopping time allowed.
